Cheerleader vs. Full Back
Alex & Leila Hormozi
Co-CEOz
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 118 --

Last year our partnership presentation
was a personal favorite of many who are
still here with us today...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 118 --

So rather than do that again, we would
like to share with you all the basics
needed in any partnership along with the
TWO relationship dynamics that we
see lead to the most success
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 118 --

We see partnerships as the foundation
we build our lives upon...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 118 --

Finances
Health/Wellness 	Relationships
Romance	Spiritual
Business
Partnership
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 118 --

Therefore, choosing the right partner and
WORKING YOUR ASS OFF can either
set you up for success...failure... or
somewhere in between
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 118 --

We see 2 dynamics that work for an
entrepreneur.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 118 --

#1 Fullback
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 118 --

This is the dynamic in which your s/o is
your chosen partner in battle. You would
literally pick this person to have your
back if an army of the walking dead were
trying to kill you
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 118 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 118 --

What does this look like??
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 118 --

- You share the same missionwww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 118 --

- You share the same mission
- You have similar interests
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 118 --

- You share the same mission
- You have similar interests
- You share the power equally
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 118 --

- You share the same mission
- You have similar interests
- You share the power equally
- You intertwine your entire lives with
one another (yes, this may be labeled
“codependent”) work, hobbies,
friends, etc...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 118 --

#2 Cheerleader
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 118 --

This dynamic is one in which your
partner may not be in the battle field
with you, but they are your #1
supporter, who without, you could
never take the insane risks you do.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 118 --

Entrepreneur 	Spouse
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 118 --

What does this look like???
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 118 --

- Your #1 fan who attends every game
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 118 --

- Your #1 fan who attends every game
- Cheerleader creates a safe place
where the other spouse can recharge
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 118 --

- Your #1 fan who attends every game
- Cheerleader creates a safe place where
the other spouse can recharge
- May have different interests but you
encourage each other in them
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 118 --

- Your #1 fan who attends every game
- Cheerleader creates a safe place where
the other spouse can recharge
- May have different interests but you
encourage each other in them
- 1 may be the bread maker while the
other has a more casual approach
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 118 --

NOTE: The Cheerleader understands
and respects your love of the game,
and would never ask you to sit out….
unless you were hurt.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 118 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 118 --

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 118 --

So… all that being said.
Can you identify which kind of
relationship you are in?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 118 --

Because here’s the truth about
relationships...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 118 --

Regardless of whichever dynamic you fit
into…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 118 --

Neither will work if there is not an
equal exchange of value.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 118 --

Spouse 1 Spouse 2
Financial Support
Family Status
Security
Unwavering Support
Takes care of the ‘nest’
Affection
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 118 --

You can give and take the same things
to each other, or different.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 118 --

The point is you give and take equally.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 33 of 118 --

And here’s a secret...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 34 of 118 --

You will always feel like you give more
than you take...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 35 of 118 --

So despite it needing to be equal from an
absolute standpoint...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 36 of 118 --

You need to let go of everything that
says “this-for-that”.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 37 of 118 --

And you have to make the decision to be
the “giver” in the relationship.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 38 of 118 --

Because we are REALLY bad at
acknowledging what other people do for
us...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 39 of 118 --

And often overestimate what we do for
others...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 40 of 118 --

So best case: both people go into it
trying to “out give” the other
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 41 of 118 --

Second best case: you go in trying to
outgive the other person and they don’t
reciprocate
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 42 of 118 --

Note: the action on your part is the
same...which is the only thing you can
control (you giving 100% to your 50%)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 43 of 118 --

What happens without this perspective?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 44 of 118 --

Resentment
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 45 of 118 --

And here’s the thing…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 46 of 118 --

Most people (and many of you in this
room) resent your spouse for things they
don’t do….
….that you have not asked for.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 47 of 118 --

IF YOU DON’T ASK THE ANSWER IS
ALWAYS NO.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 48 of 118 --

Unexpressed expectations are the
death of relationships
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 49 of 118 --

That being said...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 50 of 118 --

This is what we see the foundation for
successful relationships to be
...whether you are dynamic #1 or #2...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 51 of 118 --

Shared Mission
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 52 of 118 --

You both agree on what your goal as a
UNIT is in life.
Create a legacy?
Have a family?
Drive world change?
Inspire change in others?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 53 of 118 --

Shared Mission
Aligned Values
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 54 of 118 --

These are the values you use to make
decisions regarding each of your lives
individually and as a unit.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 55 of 118 --

….ours are the same as our team values
(we like to keep it simple ;)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 56 of 118 --

Shared Mission
Aligned Values
Clear Expectations
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 57 of 118 --

You have clarity of roles as individuals in
context of your relationship
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 58 of 118 --

You have clarity of roles as individuals
● Who makes the money?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 59 of 118 --

You have clarity of roles as individuals
● Who makes the money?
● Who takes care of the house?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 60 of 118 --

You have clarity of roles as individuals
● Who makes the money?
● Who takes care of the house?
● Who takes care of finances?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 61 of 118 --

You have clarity of roles as individuals
● Who makes the money?
● Who takes care of the house?
● Who takes care of finances?
● Who takes care of the family?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 62 of 118 --

You have clarity of roles as individuals
● Who makes the money?
● Who takes care of the house?
● Who takes care of finances?
● Who takes care of the family?
● Who maintains outside relationships with friends?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 63 of 118 --

Clarity of Roles as a Unit
THE WISHLIST / CHEAT CODES
This works so well because it gives you what we
call…
“IDEAL SCENE”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 64 of 118 --

THE WISHLIST / CHEAT CODES
In other words...what should it look like when it’s PERFECT.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 65 of 118 --

THE WISHLIST / CHEAT CODES
Example
What Alex would like from Leila….
● Daily shoulder rubs
● Leila to patiently listen to all alex’s ideas
● Daily compliments (AMAP)
● Sex at night
● Leila make all Alex food
What Leila would like from Alex….
● Daily walk together
● Alex listen to Leila without offering solution
● Tell Leila she’s pretty and in shape
● Sex in the morning
● Alex thank Leila always for tasks she does
for him
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 66 of 118 --

1) If you cannot define this for yourself, then how can you
expect the other person to guess?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 67 of 118 --

1) If you cannot define this for yourself, then how can you
expect the other person to guess?
2) If you do this with your spouse, giving them a “watered
down” version of what you want guarantees a “watered
down” version of what you want...AS THE BEST CASE!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 68 of 118 --

Which 100% guarantees not getting it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 69 of 118 --

And most people are afraid to ask for what they
REALLY want. Instead they ask for what they
think they SHOULD want.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 70 of 118 --

But you need to be secure with being vulnerable
about the things you may want from your
spouse...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 71 of 118 --

If you can’t share your authentic human
experience with your SPOUSE, who can you…??
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 72 of 118 --

Shared Mission
Aligned Values
Clear Expectations
Trust
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 73 of 118 --

Trust is built through being consistently
(and sometimes brutally) honest with
each other….
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 74 of 118 --

Honest about how you feel…
About what you need from your
spouse…
About what you are LACKING from
your spouse…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 75 of 118 --

#dontsugarcoatit
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 76 of 118 --

Shared Mission
Aligned Values
Clear Expectations
Trust
Transparency
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 77 of 118 --

“Stay in your head you’re dead” - T.R.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 78 of 118 --

Transparency is sharing your true inner
experience of life with one another,
despite fear of judgement.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 79 of 118 --

SO - Here is how we ‘see’ these
foundational pillars of a relationship...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 80 of 118 --

Shared Mission: Drive the world forward, create a legacy for your family,
etc..
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 81 of 118 --

Shared Mission: Drive the world forward, create a legacy for your family,
etc..
Aligned Values: What values do you use to run your lives?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 82 of 118 --

etc..
Aligned Values: What values do you use to run your lives?
Clear Expectations: What you do vs. what I do
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

can add whatever else you want on to it

-- 91 of 118 --

Rapidfire Q&A
(ALEX & LEILA)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 92 of 118 --

Work/Life Balance & Romance
Your Questions
● What practices do you use to "pull yourselves out" of the gym launch world and pull yourselves back into each other?
● How do you keep business and personal separate? Especially when it comes to decision making and you don’t agree.
● How do you make quality time for each other so you keep a strong loving relationship and not always talking shop?
● How much time during the work day do you spend together? Does this become conflicting with your “lanes”?
● How do you make it to where the business brings you closer together and its more fun to integrate it into your life instead of always
wanting to separate the two?
● Do you feel you do well with work and home life balance?
● Are you two going to start a family???? How has that conversation gone in the Hormozi homestead? (My wife and I are in current
negotiations for our third child and we clearly have different views on this new adventure.)
● Do you go on vacation from work?
● How do you keep a good life balance while being successful and keeping a strong relationship? Best ways to communicate it to your
man so they don’t get resentful or hurt when needing to work would be great!
● How do you meet the intimacy alive?
● How to show each other love and not make it feel like a business deal. But Growth, partnership, soul mates, understanding, balance,
and building something with foundation. Should we live by the gym launch 5 pillars?
● How much time per day do you both block off for work, and how much time per week do you block off for each other to do non work
related stuff? My wife is not part of my biz at all nor does she want to be. So I’m truly struggling with work/life balance with her and my
kids and have the ability to grow at a giant rate
● Do you use any workbooks or what tools do you all utilize in your marriage if any?
● How do you keep your work relationship separate from your home relationship?
● How do you work with your significant other/ work life balance. When do you talk to each other about work when do you stop, how do
you deal with disagreements.
● How to separate business from relationship when you are business partners and how not to talk about gym life on date night ␀␀♀
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 93 of 118 --

Work/Life Balance & Romance
Notes For Alex & Leila: Answer from both Cheerleader vs Fullback Dynamic

romantic not work? Date night?

pressure (cheerleader)

-- 100 of 118 --

Work/Life Balance & Romance
Notes For Alex & Leila: Answer from both Cheerleader vs Fullback Dynamic
● QUALITY TIME: How often? How long? What is discussed? How to keep
romantic not work? Date night?
● INTIMACY: How do you keep it alive?
● FAMILY: Having kids? Balancing kids?
● WISHLIST: What happens when one person doesn’t fulfill the other person’s
wants/needs?
● CELEBRATION: How do you celebrate big achievements? How do you do
this if the other person doesn't understand (cheerleader)?
● PRESSURE: How do you not resent your partner for not understanding the
pressure (cheerleader)
● How do you make business pull you together rather than apart?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 101 of 118 --

Decision-Making (Agreements & Disagreements)
Your Questions
● how does Alex present ideas to you so you’re able to process them, consider all angles and make an informed decision as
a business? How do you both ultimately decide which ideas are great and which ones to squash? I find I throw ideas at
Taylor that I happen to think are amazing ␀👌␀ and then I can get defensive when he criticizes them, doesn’t consider
them or doesn’t like them. He is a very calculated, strategic decision maker and I’m an all-in, big risk or go home and
figure shit out as I go type of person ␀␀♀ I know we balance each other well but I would like to come up with a process
we can use to present and consider ideas between each other. Do you have specific ways and times you present ideas to
each other?
● What advice do you have for couples who don't work together? My wife doesn't work with me and she gets really tired of
me constantly bouncing ideas off of her and filling her in on everything that's happening.
● How often and over what is your typical argument?? Who usually wins???
● With both strong personalities, how do you both don’t get “too strong” with each other? Prevent ego from leading to
comparing “who’s better”? How do you leverage on each other strengths?
● How do you decide who has the last say in business decisions if you both don’t agree on something?
● Are all business decisions made together? Or do you have certain areas where you each “have the final say”.
● When do you talk to each other about work when do you stop, how do you deal with disagreements.
● How do you communicate without hurting feelings or the other person getting resentful?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 102 of 118 --

Decision-Making (Agreements & Disagreements)www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 103 of 118 --

Decision-Making (Agreements & Disagreements)
● BIG IDEAS: How do you communicate about big ideas without hurting the other
person’s feelings? How do you know what to say yes to vs no? Set style/way of
presenting and going over the ideas? How do you not wear the other person out
with your ideas (esp if they are not in the biz?)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 104 of 118 --

Decision-Making (Agreements & Disagreements)
● BIG IDEAS: How do you communicate big ideas without hurting the other
person’s feelings? How do you know what to say yes to vs no? Set style/way of
presenting and going over the ideas? How do you not wear the other person out
with your ideas (esp if they are not in the biz?)
● FINAL SAY: Are all business decisions made together? Are there certain areas
where each person has “final say”? Who has it? What if you don’t agree?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 105 of 118 --

Decision-Making (Agreements & Disagreements)
● BIG IDEAS: How do you communicate big ideas without hurting the other
person’s feelings? How do you know what to say yes to vs no? Set style/way of
presenting and going over the ideas? How do you not wear the other person out
with your ideas (esp if they are not in the biz?)
● FINAL SAY: Are all business decisions made together? Are there certain areas
where each person has “final say”? Who has it? What if you don’t agree?
● DISAGREEMENTS: How often do you argue? Over what? Who wins?
Follow-up: how do you table it so it doesn’t ruin the rest of the day/week when
you don’t come to an agreement?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 106 of 118 --

Decision-Making (Agreements & Disagreements)
● BIG IDEAS: How do you communicate big ideas without hurting the other
person’s feelings? How do you know what to say yes to vs no? Set style/way of
presenting and going over the ideas? How do you not wear the other person out
with your ideas (esp if they are not in the biz?)
● FINAL SAY: Are all business decisions made together? Are there certain areas
where each person has “final say”? Who has it? What if you don’t agree?
● DISAGREEMENTS: How often do you argue? Over what? Who wins?
Follow-up: how do you table it so it doesn’t ruin the rest of the day/week when
you don’t come to an agreement?
● ALPHA: With both strong personalities, how do you both don’t get “too strong”
with each other? Prevent ego from leading to comparing “who’s better”? How do
you leverage on each other strengths?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 107 of 118 --

Roles
Your Questions
● How do you hold each other accountable in business without making things sour? How do you make sure you are both crisp -
and that you are able to count on each other o follow through, initiative and innovation.
● How did you decide who does what when you started your business?
● How do you hold each other accountable for what needs to be completed without constantly asking if things are completed, do
you guys have a “staff” meeting between the two of you?
● What happens when someone doesn’t fulfill the others weekly relationship “wants/responsibilities” list?
● So for those of us with an other (fiancée) who has their own job and isn’t involved at all in the business... how do you not resent
them when you’re feeling pressure but they don’t understand. Also, when a huge goal is achieved and you want to celebrate it,
how do deal when they don’t understand what a huge deal it is. My fiancée is Chinese and cultures and perception is very
different for her.
● Leila Hormozi also how to improve our own personable accountability to our routines and structures. A practical tactic.
● Beyond the marriage superset, how do you two manage and optimize your projects, schedules, todo lists etc.?
● How bad do you want to stick your face on the door glass in the background when Alex makes vids during his 5 hour block?
😂But really, do you have your own block set aside for yourself like Alex does? If so is it another part of the day or same hours?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 108 of 118 --

Roles
● ACCOUNTABILITY: How to hold each other accountable? What if things
don’t get done?
○ How do you hold ourselves accountable--tactics
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

don’t get done?

● DIVISION OF LABOR: Who does what? How did you decide?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Roles

don’t get done?
○ How do you hold ourselves accountable--tactics
● DIVISION OF LABOR: Who does what? How did you decide?
● COMM CADENCE: Do you have staff meeting between the two of you?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 111 of 118 --

Roles
● ACCOUNTABILITY: How to hold each other accountable? What if things
don’t get done?
○ How do you hold yourselves accountable--tactics
● DIVISION OF LABOR: Who does what? How did you decide?
● COMM CADENCE: Do you have staff meeting between the two of you?
● CREATIVE TIME: Leila - do you give yourself work blocks of creative
time like alex?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 112 of 118 --

Misc
Your Questions
● How do you create boundaries with clients without sacrificing service and attention? What are the common red flags you
DON'T want a prospect in your gym? Like a page of "deal breakers" we can use or provide to our Sales Manager to avoid
energy vampires and soul sucking negative nancies.
● When you had no money. And you started making money. Did you burn through it fast ending up at ground zero again.
How did you break that cycle.
● When you have a cheat meal, do you take turns on who chooses? Or do You each get what you want?
● Do you live by the core tenets?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 113 of 118 --

Misc
● VALUES: Do you live by the core tenets?

your income increased?

your income increased?
● CLIENT BOUNDARIES: How do you identify shitty clients and protect
yourselves from them? How do you know when to kick someone out?
Can you ID them at the sale?

TRUST & LOYALTY
GO ALL IN ONCE AN AGREEMENT IS MADE
